content: After a couple years of odd jobbing and... wandering in Germany, I finally returned to the United States in 1992 and immediately spent the next five years wandering, hitchhiking, mostly on the west coast of the United States, most of that in the state of California. Needless to say, the return to the States was yet another culture shock for me. I say yet another because I regard the entry into public high school, my... my... making my way to Germany and coming back from Germany to the States as three major instances of what you might call culture shock to my otherwise existence. Since having returned to the States, I have spent most of the intervening years either wandering or, for short periods, trying to hold down a job and lead what most would consider a normal existence with a roof over my head.
